news.headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE: HPE) to host live webcast of Q1 fiscal 2025 earnings conference call on March 6 at 4:00 p.m. CT.
news.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has announced a live audio webcast of an upcoming conference call with analysts to discuss the financial results of the first quarter of fiscal year 2025, which ended on January 31, 2025. The call is set for Thursday, March 6, at 4:00 p.m. CT (5:00 p.m. ET). Interested parties can access the webcast at www.hpe.com/investor/2025Q1Webcast. Following the call, a replay will be posted on the same website for approximately.

news.headline: All Eyes on Trump - FJELite
BounceBit Mainnet v1.6.1 boosts performance and security with architecture upgrades for increased efficiency and platform compatibility.
news.summary: The latest version, BounceBit Mainnet v1.6.1, has been rolled out with significant architectural enhancements pertaining to performance, security, and platform compatibility. These improvements aim to boost the blockchain's efficacy and resilience.

news.headline: BNB Chain reveals 2025 plans: Cut transaction time, eliminate gas fees, fight malicious MEV, and integrate AI for Web3. (Read more)
news.summary: The 2025 roadmap for BNB Chain is centered around key goals, including decreasing transaction latency, eliminating gas fees entirely, addressing malicious MEV activity, and incorporating artificial intelligence to transform the way Web3 operates. These initiatives aim to enhance the user experience and streamline interactions on the platform.

news.headline: Federal buyout takers fall short, raising chances of mass layoffs.
BNB Chain hosts AI hackathon to spur blockchain innovation. Participants eligible for prizes, guidance, and visibility.
news.summary: An ongoing AI hackathon has been launched by BNB Chain, aiming to promote innovation at the intersection of AI and blockchain technologies. The hackathon is designed to encourage participants to create new solutions by leveraging AI on the blockchain platform. Contestants stand a chance to win valuable prizes, receive mentorship from experts in the field, and gain exposure for their work. This initiative by BNB Chain seeks to drive adoption of AI within the realm of blockchain technology.

news.headline: $120M from Ethereum Foundation allocated to DeFi apps; community cheers as 45,000 ETH transferred to Aave, Spark, Compound.
news.summary: Members of the community rejoiced as the Ethereum Foundation allocated 45,000 ETH to decentralized finance platforms such as Aave, Spark, and Compound.

news.headline: Microbix reports strong Q1 fiscal 2025 with $6M+ revenues, 40% YoY growth, $0.9M net earnings, $150K royalties, 62% margin.
news.summary: Microbix Biosystems Inc. reported a 40% year-over-year increase in recurring sales in its first quarter of fiscal 2025 ended December 31, 2024. Total revenue for the quarter was $6,044,002, with antigen revenues at $4,266,758 (up 118% from the prior year) and QAPs revenues at $1,626,980. The company achieved a net income of $856,962 without any Kinlytic-related revenues. Operating expenses decreased by 23%, leading to improved margins. Cash provided by operating activities was $792,702. Microbix's current ratio was 6.62, and its debt-to-equity ratio was 0.36 at the end of Q1. The company aims for annual sales growth in the range of 20 to 40%.

news.headline: PHINIA Inc. (NYSE: PHIN) reports Q4 2024 net sales of $833 million, down 5.6% from Q4 2023. Full-year results show a 2.9% decrease.
news.summary: PHINIA Inc. (NYSE: PHIN), a top provider of premium fuel systems, electrical systems, and aftermarket solutions based in Auburn Hills, Mich., has released its financial results for Q4 and full year ending December 31, 2024. In the fourth quarter, the company posted net sales of $833 million, marking a 5.6% decline from Q4 2023. While Fuel Systems (FS) sales decreased in all regions, robust Aftermarket sales across the board helped offset the decline, resulting in a modest year-over-year decrease of 2.9% after adjustments.

news.headline: Kelly reported Q4 revenue of $1.2 billion, down 3.3% YoY, but up 4.4% organically. Full-year revenue was $4.3 billion, down 10.4% but up 0.5% organically. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $43.5 million, up 34%, and for the year was $143.5 million, up 31%. The company expects growth in fiscal 2025. CEO Peter Quigley plans to retire by the end of 2025.
news.summary: Kelly, a specialty talent solutions provider, announced Q4 2024 revenue of $1.2 billion, down 3.3% YOY due to dispositions and acquisitions, but up 4.4% organically. Full-year revenue was $4.3 billion, down 10.4% as reported and up 0.5% organically. Q4 operating loss was $56.7 million, adjusted operating income was $29.2 million, EBITDA was $43.5 million, and adjusted EBITDA margin was 3.7%. Full-year operating loss was $15.1 million, adjusted EBITDA was $143.5 million, and adjusted EBITDA margin was 3.3%. The company expects growth in 2025. CEO Peter Quigley plans to retire by year-end 2025. The company's board declared a dividend of $0.075 per share. Kelly will host a live webcast to discuss the results.

news.headline: Yellow Pages Limited reported Q4 2024 revenues of $51.4M, Adjusted EBITDA of $8.2M, and net income of $2.7M. Declared $0.25 dividend per share for March 17, 2025. Initiatives show progress in revenue stability and customer acquisitions with a cash balance of approximately $49M. Pension plan payments completed.
news.summary: Yellow Pages Limited reported their operating and financial results for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2024. The company is pleased with their progress in revenue stability, increased profitability, and healthy cash balance. Quarterly revenues were $51.4 million compared to $55.9 million in 2023, with year-end revenues at $214.8 million down from $239.4 million in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA was $8.2 million for the quarter and $50.8 million for the year. Net income was $2.7 million in the quarter and $25 million for the year. A quarterly dividend of $0.25 per common share was declared, to be paid on March 17, 2025. The company made $1.5 million of voluntary payments towards their Defined Benefit Pension Plan's wind-up deficit in Q4 2024. They also reported a cash balance of approximately $49 million at the end of January.

news.headline: Global bunion surgery market to reach US$606.1 million by 2029 with 5.7% CAGR driven by rising bunions prevalence and aging population. Dominated by implants & accessories segment.Traditional surgeries hold largest share due to proven efficacy.
news.summary: The global Bunion Surgery Market, valued at US$437.8 million in 2023, is expected to grow at a CAGR of 5.7%, reaching US$458.4 million in 2024 and US$606.1 million by 2029. The market is primarily driven by the rising prevalence of bunions, an aging population more prone to orthopedic issues like bunions, and the increasing adoption of minimally invasive procedures with shorter recovery times. Another growth factor is the rise in people with rheumatoid arthritis, leading to joint deformities like bunions. Implants & accessories segment dominated the market in 2023. Traditional surgeries hold a larger market share due to proven efficacy, especially in severe cases. The market is expected to continue its steady growth over the coming years.

news.headline: Global medical device connectivity market to reach $18.86B by 2035, with a 23.5% CAGR. North America leads with $2.29B in 2025.
news.summary: A new report by Fact.MR predicts that the global medical device connectivity market will be valued at $2.29 billion in 2025 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 23.5% from 2025 to 2035. The rise in adoption of electronic health record systems, allowing seamless communication between medical devices and EHR systems, is a key driver for this market growth. North America leads the market due to its advanced healthcare infrastructure and digital health technology adoption. The global market is projected to reach $18.86 billion by 2035, with the wireless medical device connectivity segment expected to reach $12.35 billion by then. Specific figures include the US market estimating $550.77 million revenue in 2025 and Japan's market expanding at a 25% CAGR from 2025 to 2035. The hospitals and clinics segment is forecasted to hold 38.9% of the market revenue share by 2035. East Asia's market is also expected to grow at a 23.8% CAGR through 2035.

news.headline: Dynacor Group's January 2025 gold sales hit $28.7 million, a 6.7% increase from January 2024's $26.9 million.
news.summary: Montreal-based Dynacor Group Inc. (TSX-DNG) reported unaudited gold sales totaling $28.7 million in January. This marks a significant increase from the previous year, with sales in January 2024 totaling $26.9 million. The $1.8 million growth represents a 6.7% increase year over year. It is important to note that all figures are reported in US dollars, unless specified otherwise. The January 2025 gold sales of $28.7 million equate to approximately C$41.3 million. This achievement marks the second-highest monthly sales for Dynacor.

news.headline: Organon's Q4 & full-year earnings for 2024 announced; period ending December 31, 2024.
news.summary: Jersey City, New Jersey - Organon has reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024, which concluded on December 31, 2024.

news.headline: Nova (NVMI) posts record Q4 revenue of $194.8 million, up 9% from previous quarter, surpassing company guidance.
news.summary: Israeli company Nova (Nasdaq: NVMI) based in Rehovot, reported financial results for Q4 and full year 2024. Q4 highlights include a record revenue of $194.8 million, showing a 9% increase from the previous quarter, surpassing company guidance.

news.headline: Porter Capital gives $15M financing to new transportation startup in Georgia. Trusted leader in specialty financing.
news.summary: A company based in Georgia operating in the transportation and freight services industry has received a $15 million credit facility from Porter Capital, a renowned provider of specialty financing solutions based in Birmingham, Alabama. The recipient, a newly established entity, will benefit from the financial support as it seeks to grow and expand its operations.

news.headline: V2X Inc. secures top spot on GSA's OASIS+ contract across 8 domains, cements role as industry leader.
news.summary: In Reston, Virginia on February 13, 2025, V2X Inc. (NYSE: VVX) secured a spot on the General Services Administration's (GSA) OASIS+ contract in all eight domains. This achievement cements V2X Inc.'s position as a top service provider.

news.headline: Palatin reports Q2 financials, completes phase 2 obesity study with bremelanotide and tirzepatide. Topline data soon. New MC4R peptide and oral molecule being developed.
news.summary: A study on co-administration of MC4R agonist bremelanotide and GLP-1/GIP dual agonist tirzepatide in Phase 2 obesity treatment has completed database lock. The topline data is anticipated to be released later this month. This study involved a new 'next generation' selective MC4R long-acting peptide and an oral small molecule. Multiple clinical trials were conducted as part of this research.

news.headline: Canopy Growth introduces 2 new flavors, Rocket Root Beer and Charged Cream Soda, to its High-Potency Cannabis Beverage line.
news.summary: Deep Space Propulsion is leading the way in the high-potency cannabis beverage category with its new flavors Blue Sourberry and Altitude Lemon Lime. The company, based in Smiths Falls, ON, announced this expansion of its innovative portfolio on Feb. 13, 2025. Canopy Growth Corporation, the parent company, is at the forefront of this movement.

(NYSE: HOUS), a prominent player in residential real estate services worldwide, shared its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year concluding on December 31, 2024. news.headline: Realbotix Corp. (TSX-V: XBOT) reports Q1-2025 revenue up by 419% to $858k. Humanoid robot & AI creator.
news.summary: Realbotix Corp. (TSX-V: XBOT) (Frankfurt Stock Exchange: 76M0.F) (OTC: XBOTF), a top developer of humanoid robots and AI companions, has released its interim financial report for the quarter ending December 31, 2024. During this period, the company saw a significant 419% surge in total revenue, reaching $858,000. All financial figures are in United States dollars.

news.headline: Lincoln Electric's Q4 2024 net income $140.2M, EPS $2.47, with special item charges. Prior year net income $156.6M, EPS $2.70.
news.summary: In Cleveland, Lincoln Electric Holdings, Inc. announced today its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. The company reported a net income of $140.2 million, with diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $2.47. This figure includes special item after-tax net charges amounting to $5.8 million, or $0.10 EPS. Comparatively, the same period in the previous year saw a net income of $156.6 million, or $2.70 EPS, including special item after-tax net gains of $14.6 million, or $0.25 EPS. Adjusted for special items, the fourth quarter of 2024 showed an improved performance. The company's stock is listed on the Nasdaq under the symbol LECO.

news.headline: CyberArk partners with Builder.ai to secure identities in the cloud, leveraging intelligent privilege controls. Builder.ai's AI platform accelerates app development.
news.summary: In Newton, Mass. and Petach Tikva, Israel, CyberArk (NASDAQ: CYBR) has partnered with Builder.ai to enhance identity security across its diverse cloud infrastructure. By leveraging CyberArk's solutions, Builder.ai aims to safeguard and streamline user access to applications for its workforce and developers. The innovative composable software platform offered by Builder.ai, powered by AI, enables swift development of web and mobile applications. This collaboration signifies CyberArk's commitment to providing intelligent privilege controls in protecting identities within Builder.ai's operational ecosystem.

news.headline: Germany requests exemptions from gas storage filling target

Honda Motor Co., Ltd. reported operating profit of 1,139.9 billion Yen with an operating margin of 7.0% in fiscal Q3 2024. Motorcycle sales exceeded 15.5 million units, but automobile sales dropped by 297,000.
news.summary: Today, Honda Motor Co., Ltd. disclosed its financial performance for the fiscal third quarter ending on December 31, 2024. The company reported an operating profit of 1,139.9 billion Yen, with an operating margin of 7.0%. During the nine-month period from April to December 2024, the motorcycle business saw Unit Sales surpass 15,508,000, driven by robust global sales. Conversely, the automobile business experienced a decrease of 297,000 in Unit Sales. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Zebra Technologies exceeded Q4 expectations, with higher guidance for the current quarter, but cautious 2025 outlook caused stock to fall.
news.summary: Zebra Technologies exceeded expectations in the fourth quarter and provided a higher guidance for the current quarter. However, despite this positive news, the stock of Zebra fell due to its cautious outlook for 2025. The company is optimistic about its performance in the near future, with strong numbers in Q4. They aim to continue this positive trend in the upcoming quarter. Their 2025 outlook, while more conservative, indicates a strategic approach towards future growth. This information was reported by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Olympic champ Jessie Diggins focuses on improvement, not winning, but continues to win races.
news.summary: Olympic gold medalist skier Jessie Diggins, known for setting goals like many champions, focuses on continuous improvement rather than solely on winning. While achieving success, her primary aim remains enhancing her skills and performance. This mindset has led to her consistent victories, showcasing her dedication to growth in the sport.

news.headline: John Wooden and Pat Summitt faced setbacks but rebounded to achieve success in basketball coaching.
news.summary: Renowned basketball coaches John Wooden and Pat Summitt reached remarkable success in their careers, facing significant challenges along the way. Despite setbacks, they rebounded with resilience, embodying the spirit of champions. Their inspiring stories serve as a testament to overcoming adversity and achieving greatness in the world of basketball. This article highlights how triumphing over difficulties can lead to unparalleled success and is featured on Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: SharkNinja exceeds Q4 estimates, raises 2025 targets, leading to stock increase. Investor's Business Daily reports.
news.summary: Manufacturer of kitchen and household appliances, SharkNinja, exceeded expectations for the fourth quarter and provided optimistic guidance for the future. This positive news resulted in a rise in SharkNinja's stock. The company's performance has led to raised targets for the year 2025.

news.headline: Datadog beats Q4 earnings estimates, but stock falls on weak 2025 revenue outlook. Stock tumbles on lower revenue forecast for 2025.
news.summary: Datadog's stock experienced a decline following the release of its Q4 earnings report, which surpassed estimates. However, the company's full-year revenue guidance for 2025 fell short of expectations. This news resulted in a drop in the software stock.

news.headline: Blackline Safety names Paul Semak as VP of North American Sales, bringing 25+ years of experience. Accelerating growth strategy.
news.summary: In Calgary, Canada, Blackline Safety Corp. (TSX: BLN) has appointed Paul Semak as the Vice President of North American Sales. Bringing over 25 years of sales leadership experience and expertise in SaaS, cloud solutions, and enterprise technology, Semak will drive Blackline's sales strategy. With a background that includes over five years as Senior Vice President of Americ, Semak's goal is to propel market expansion and growth for the company.

news.headline: Evercore's Emanuel disagrees that the bull market is finished.

Brookfield Wealth Solutions reported $140.46M in total assets, $12.87M in adjusted equity, $1.374B in distributable operating earnings, and $1.247B in net income for the year ended December 31, 2024. The company completed the acquisition of AEL, deployed $17B across investments, and generated $19B in annuity and PRT sales. Quarterly distribution increased to $0.09 per share. Brookfield Corporation reported $101M in net income and $1.606B in distributable earnings for the quarter ended December 31, 2024.
news.summary: Brookfield Wealth Solutions reported their financial results for the three months and year ended December 31, 2024. The total assets surged to $140.46 billion from $61.64 billion in 2023, with adjusted equity at $12.87 billion. Net income stood at $576 million for the three months and $1.2 billion for the year. They completed the acquisition of AEL, doubled their business size, and deployed over $17 billion in investments. The annuity and pension risk transfer sales reached $19 billion, including the UK reinsurance deal worth $1.3 billion. They declared a 13% increase in quarterly return of capital, reaching $0.09 per class A share and class B share. Brookfield Corporation's net income was $432 million, with distributable earnings at $1.6 billion. Their liquidity position is robust, with $31 billion in cash and $21 billion in long-term liquid investments, supporting higher yielding investment strategies. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Ceres Global Ag Corp. reported a net loss of $379k for the quarter ended Dec 31, 2024, with revenue of $219.9 million. CEO mentioned macroeconomic challenges.
news.summary: Ceres Global Ag Corp. reported its financial results for the six-month period ended December 31, 2024. In the quarter ended December 31, 2024, the company experienced a net loss of $379,000 compared to income of $2.7 million in the same quarter of the previous year. Gross profit for the quarter was $3.8 million, down from $7.9 million the previous year. The corporation closed the sale of its Beausejour facility in Manitoba, resulting in a gain of $316,000. Tom Coyle was appointed as the Interim President and CEO. The company also made amendments to its revolving credit and term loan facilities. Despite economic challenges, Coyle remains positive on the company's performance, noting strong volumes in their joint venture and supply chain services. Revenue for the quarter was $219,953, down from $282,200 in the previous year, while net income was a loss of $379,000 compared to income of $2,670,000 in the same quarter of 2023. Adjusted net income was a loss of $379,000 for the quarter compared to $2,734,000 in the previous year.

news.headline: Ceva, Inc. reports Q4 revenue of $29.2 million (up 21% YoY) with 623 million units shipped - FY2024 revenue $106.93 million (up 10%), 2 billion Ceva-powered units shipped, GAAP loss $0.37 per share, Non-GAAP earnings $0.36 per share. Company optimistic about future growth.
news.summary: Ceva, Inc. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter ending December 31, 2024. Total revenue for the quarter was $29.2 million, up by 21% year-over-year, with royalty revenue reaching $13.5 million. They also noted the shipment of a record high of 623 million Ceva-powered units, a 38% increase year-over-year. For the full year 2024, total revenue was $106.9 million, a 10% increase, with royalty revenue of $46.9 million, up by 18%. Ceva signed 43 license agreements in 2024 and shipped 2 billion Ceva-powered smart edge devices. GAAP loss per share was $0.37. Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share doubled to $0.36 year-over-year. The company also completed share repurchases in 2024. Ceva's CEO, Amir Panush, highlighted the strong quarter and successful partnerships driving long-term royalty streams. The CFO, Yaniv Arieli, emphasized the revenue growth and doubled non-GAAP EPS in 2024. Ceva will host a conference call to discuss their performance and future plans. The press release includes forward-looking statements and details of non-GAAP financial measures used in the report. Ceva is a leading licensor of silicon and software IP specializing in Smart Edge devices.

news.headline: Crocs, Inc. announced record 2024 results with $4.1 billion in annual revenue, up 4% from 2023. Adjusted diluted EPS increased by 9% to $13.17. Crocs expects another year of revenue growth in 2025, upsizing share repurchase authorization by $1 billion, totaling $1.3 billion. Operating margin for 2025 is estimated at 24%, with a focus on sustained growth and value creation.Revenue for Q4 2024 was $990 million, up 3.1%, with an operating margin of 20.2%. Crocs Brand growth was positive, with North America and China performing well. Full-year 2024 results saw revenue growth of 3.5%, Operating margin of 24.9%, and diluted EPS of $15.88, up 24.2%. Crocs repaid $323 million of debt in 2024 and repurchased shares worth $551 million. Crocs posted a net income of $368,909 in Q4 2024. Cash and cash equivalents were at $180 million at the end of December 2024. Crocs expects revenue to be down about 3.5% in the first quarter of 2025 and revenue growth for the full year 2025 at around 2% to 2.5%. Adjusted operating margin for 2025 is projected to be around 24% with adjusted diluted EPS guidance varying between $12.70 to $13.15. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.summary: Crocs, Inc. reported a successful full-year 2024 with a 24% increase in diluted EPS to $15.88 and a 9% increase in adjusted diluted EPS to $13.17. Their revenue grew by 4% to $4.1 billion. The company repurchased over $550 million in shares and paid down $320 million of debt. The brand is expecting continued revenue growth in 2025, with an emphasis on the Crocs Brand and an upsize in share repurchase authorization by $1 billion. In Q4 2024, consolidated revenues were $990 million, with gross margin at 57.9%, and diluted EPS at $6.36. For the full year, revenues were $4.1 billion, gross margin at 58.8%, adjusted operating margin at 24.9%, and adjusted diluted EPS at $13.17. Shares were repurchased for $551 million during the year, with $324 million of repurchase authorization remaining. The company projects adjusted operating margin of 21.5% for Q1 2025 and revenue growth of 2% to 2.5% for the full year. Their diluted EPS guidance for 2025 is $12.70 to $13.15. Crocs, Inc. upped its share repurchase authorization to $1.3 billion.

news.headline: Navios Maritime Partners L.P. reported Q4 2024 revenue of $332.5M, net income of $94.7M, and $0.05 per unit cash distribution. FY 2024 revenue was $1,334.1M and net income $367.3M. EBITDA: $197.6M for Q4 2024, $757.4M for FY 2024. Cash distribution was $0.20 per unit annualized. 489,955 common units repurchased for $25M and 585,420 units for $29.2M. Sales included $18.8M from two bulk vessels and $25.4M for one ultra-handymax. Total contracted revenue was $3.6B as of February 2025. Earnings per common unit were $3.11 for Q4 2024 and $11.98 for FY 2024. Navios took delivery of four newbuilding vessels. Budget updates and non-GAAP financial measures were provided. Fleet employment, financial, and balance sheet data were shared. Conference call details were given. Navios Maritime Partners is an international owner and operator of dry cargo and tanker vessels.
news.summary: Navios Maritime Partners L.P. reported significant financial results for the fourth quarter and year ended December 31, 2024. In Q4 2024, revenue was $332.5 million, with a net income of $94.7 million. For FY 2024, revenue reached $1.33 billion, with net income at $367.3 million. Earnings per common unit were $3.11 for Q4 and $11.98 for the year. Net cash from operating activities was $114.9 million for Q4 and $483.5 million for FY 2024. EBITDA stood at $197.6 million for Q4 and $757.4 million for the year. The company repurchased common units worth $25.0 million in 2024. They reported $3.6 billion in contracted revenue as of February 2025. Navios Partners also provided detailed updates on vessel sales, purchases, and financing agreements. Overall, the company exhibited growth and stability despite global economic uncertainties.

news.headline: NFN8 Group boosts Bitcoin mining operations with upgrades in Texas, Iowa, expanding to a new 450,000 sq ft facility.

news.headline: Sidetrade and Interpath form alliance to boost digital transformation with AI-powered Order-to-Cash applications.
news.summary: Sidetrade, a prominent company specializing in AI-driven Order-to-Cash solutions, has partnered with Interpath, an international consulting firm. This alliance aims to expedite digital transformation initiatives by enabling businesses to leverage AI technology from Sidetrade's specialized Order-to-Cash Data Lake. This collaboration is geared towards aiding organizations in adapting efficiently to the evolving demands of the quickly changing economy.
